spec_type_rationale: pre-seeded from research handoff at research/2026-06-27-remove-leftover-electron-vite/handoff.json (user accepted)

findings.content: Never commit secrets or debug artifacts (console.log, debugger, stray print) — directly mandates removing the ipcMain.on('ping', () => console.log('pong')) handler
Every change impacts as little code as possible; never 'fix' unrelated code — constrains fix to deleting the single dead statement + its comment, no scope expansion
Main process (src/main/) is the IPC host and may use Node/Electron freely; ipcMain registration belongs to this layer — removal stays within the main layer, no cross-layer impact
Never commit debug artifacts — no console.log, debugger, print() left behind; the ping/pong handler is exactly this artifact
Minimal changes — every change should impact as little code as possible; supports the delete-only approach over replace-with-real-IPC
bugs/002-leftover-ping-debug-artifact.md is the source record (Status Open, Severity Info); lifecycle Open to Fixed is manual — spec should drive it to resolution
Main process (Node.js) owns app lifecycle and creates the single BrowserWindow with preload attached; it is the IPC host — the ping handler lives in this entry, src/main/index.ts
No test infrastructure exists for the main or preload processes; verification of the removal is build/typecheck/smoke, not an automated main-process test
main may use Node/Electron freely and never imports renderer code; removing a dead ipcMain handler does not affect preload or renderer boundaries

src/main/index.ts is the sole main-process entry file; drives ready/activate/window-all-closed lifecycle — the documented purpose does NOT include any 'ping' IPC handler, confirming it as undocumented dead scaffold
main concern is a single file (src/main/index.ts); the change touches exactly this one file
Doc omission: the ping/pong handler is absent from the documented main-process responsibilities, so no docs/ update is needed after removal
Symptom site: ipcMain.on('ping', () => console.log('pong')) at src/main/index.ts:54, preceded by // IPC test comment at line 53; sole ipcMain handler in src/
Verified dead: 0 ipcRenderer/send/invoke callers across src/preload + src/renderer; runner-up 'live IPC contract' framing disproven
Delete the handler + its // IPC test comment, no replacement; createWindow and app.whenReady boot flow must stay untouched (unchanged_behavior)
Verify cost Low: grep confirms 'ping' gone, typecheck + build pass, app boots; risk Low (no behavioral surface)

decision_points.description: After deleting the handler, ipcMain becomes an unused import on line 1; remove ipcMain from the import too, or leave it
decision_points.user_answer: Remove ipcMain from the line 1 import alongside the handler — keeps lint/typecheck passing

decision_points.description: no relevant decision point for existing_behavior
no relevant decision point for data_flow_state
no relevant decision point for edge_cases
no relevant decision point for ui_ux_details
no relevant decision point for breaking_changes
no relevant decision point for tooling_configuration

mandatory_reads.n_a_reason: src/main/index.ts imports only framework/vendor (electron, path, @electron-toolkit/utils, resources/icon asset); no first-party project dependency is touched
src/main/index.ts is the main-process entry module (CBM in_degree 0); it has no callers

overview: Remove the leftover electron-vite scaffold debug handler from the Electron main process. The bug (bugs/002-leftover-ping-debug-artifact.md) is a dead no-op IPC handler — ipcMain.on('ping', () => console.log('pong')) — that violates the project's no-debug-artifacts rule. This spec deletes the handler, its // IPC test comment, and the now-unused ipcMain import.
current_state: src/main/index.ts:54 registers ipcMain.on('ping', () => console.log('pong')) inside the app.whenReady().then() block, preceded by a // IPC test comment at src/main/index.ts:53. ipcMain is imported on src/main/index.ts:1 (import { app, shell, BrowserWindow, ipcMain } from 'electron') and is used ONLY at line 54. Research (research/2026-06-27-remove-leftover-electron-vite.md) confirmed via CBM search_code that this is the sole ipcMain handler in src/ and that zero ipcRenderer/send/invoke callers exist across src/preload and src/renderer — the handler is provably dead. git log shows it dates from the initial scaffold commit (8ff7b01). docs/main/index.md documents the main-process responsibilities (lifecycle + window creation) and does not mention any ping handler. No test infrastructure exists for the main process (docs/architecture.md).
desired_behavior: src/main/index.ts no longer registers the 'ping' IPC handler: the ipcMain.on('ping', ...) statement (line 54) and its preceding // IPC test comment (line 53) are deleted, and ipcMain is removed from the line 1 electron import so no unused import remains. The app boot flow is otherwise unchanged — createWindow, app.whenReady, the browser-window-created/activate/window-all-closed handlers, and BrowserWindow creation all behave exactly as before. No console output is produced for a 'ping' message because the handler no longer exists. typecheck, lint, and build all pass.

affected_areas.impact: Delete the ipcMain.on('ping', ...) handler (line 54) + its // IPC test comment (line 53); remove ipcMain from the line 1 electron import

acceptance_criteria.statement: The mintenvoy main process shall not register a 'ping' IPC handler.
WHEN the application starts, the mintenvoy main process shall create the BrowserWindow and complete its ready-event lifecycle exactly as before the change.
WHEN the main process initializes, the mintenvoy main process shall register no 'ping' IPC listener and emit no 'pong' log message.
The main process entry file shall contain no console-log debug artifact.
The mintenvoy codebase shall pass lint and type-check with no unused import remaining in the main process entry file.
acceptance_criteria.verification_command: npm run lint && npm run typecheck

ac_subsection_na.ci_pipeline: No CI/pipeline changes — source-only dead-code removal in src/main/index.ts
ac_subsection_na.hooks_gates: No hooks or gates added or changed by this removal
ac_subsection_na.documentation: docs/main/index.md does not document the ping handler; no docs/ update is required after removal

out_of_scope.content: Replacing the dead handler with a real application IPC handler (research approach 2 — no current IPC need)
Adding test infrastructure for the main process to cover this removal (none exists today)
Any docs/ changes — the ping handler is undocumented, so no documentation update is needed
Manually updating the bugs/002-leftover-ping-debug-artifact.md record lifecycle (Open to Fixed is a manual step outside this spec)
Touching renderer or preload code, or any other ipcMain handler (none exist; ping is the sole undocumented dead scaffold)

constraints.content: App boot and window-creation flow (createWindow, the app ready event, browser-window-created, activate, and window-all-closed handlers) must not regress
Keep the change minimal: delete only the dead handler, its // IPC test comment, and the now-unused ipcMain import — do not modify unrelated main-process code

risks.risk: Deleting the handler accidentally removes a still-needed import or alters the whenReady block, breaking app boot
risks.mitigation: Remove only ipcMain from the line 1 import (other imports stay); typecheck + lint + build gate the change; smoke-launch confirms the window still opens
